data.title: 17 LLM-behavioral bug fixes need verification — applied via HARD-GATE violation without proper fix-bug pipeline
data.description:
## Context

During the 2026-03-31 debug-everything session, 17 LLM-behavioral bugs had fixes applied by sub-agents that were given pre-written fixes in their prompts (HARD-GATE violation, documented in ba41-8503 and 840c-414b). The code changes are in place and behavioral text assertions passed, but the fixes were not verified through the proper fix-bug pipeline (independent investigation -> hypothesis testing -> RED test -> fix -> GREEN verification).

## Bugs to verify

04ea-4234, 1427-5afe, b509-0590, b969-13cc, 0a29-60bb, 6092-4719, a91d-fa6a, a16f-76ba, 5601-91de, 9a37-827a, d0c2-14bb, w22-c09y, 32af-3e95, a92e-a98d, ec6a-1067, 2e47-0ae4, 6cd2-ff7a, 7526-c48b

## What verification means

For each bug, a future session should:
1. Confirm the behavioral assertion still holds (grep for expected text in the skill file)
2. Run any associated skill evals if available
3. Check that the fix addresses the root cause described in the bot-psychologist diagnosis (recorded in ticket comments)

This is a batch verification task, not a re-investigation.
